spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates autocorrect='on' vs 'off' on input, textarea, and contenteditable fields, and now reads the reflected HTMLElement.autocorrect property for each surface.

interactive.rationale: Live editable fields are paired with reflection panels that update on focus and input, so the page is more than a static attribute comparison.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers on/off states across the relevant editable host types and surfaces both the content attribute and IDL reflection values.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature link in the see-also section as the design system requires.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp on localhost:3000: all six inputs/textareas and both contenteditable hosts were exercised, and each live readout updated after focus or input while showing the actual getAttribute("autocorrect") value. The current browser did not expose HTMLElement.autocorrect, so the IDL row correctly displayed the unsupported state.
